pattern: Slide a fixed-size window of length k across the array, maintaining its running sum to find the largest window sum, then divide by k.
hints: Every candidate subarray has the same length k, so compare their sums directly.
Compute the first window's sum, then slide by adding the entering element and removing the leaving one.
Track the maximum window sum and return it divided by k as a floating value.

edge_inputs.description: window in the middle holds the best average
single element with k equal to one
k equals the whole array length
all negative values, least-negative window wins
k of one selects the single max element
extreme magnitudes at the value bounds
best window is at the very start
best window is at the very end

stress.description: large random array with a random valid window size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nums = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(size)]
    k = rng.randint(1, size)
    return [nums, k]
